title: rubus australis var. schmidelioides
intro: Rubus australis var. schmidelioides, also known as the Tasmanian raspberry, is a variety of the Rubus australis species of bramble plants native to Tasmania. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, and has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Rubus australis var. schmidelioides include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion for its medicinal purposes, and is also used in herbal preparations such as salves and ointments.
